meta.title: depa Smart City Leadership
meta.description: Smart City Leadership by depa: official cohort details, alumni directory, and a multilingual archive for Thailand's smart-city executive program.

text.open: Cohort {{cohort}} is accepting applications through {{deadline}} on depa's official announcement page.
text.closed: Cohort {{cohort}} applications have closed. Review the official depa page for the course structure and source details.
text.inSession: Cohort {{cohort}} is currently underway. The official depa page remains the source of record for the program details.
text.completed: Cohort {{cohort}} has concluded. The official depa page remains the source of record for the program overview.

hero.title: Smart City Leadership
hero.valueStatement: A serious short-format program for leaders who need sharper judgment about how smart-city work actually gets delivered.
hero.subtitle: SCL is most useful when read not as a promotional course page, but as a compact operating brief: it helps public and private leaders understand the strategic, institutional, and implementation logic behind credible smart-city projects in Thailand.
hero.imageCaption: The strongest value is what happens when the cohort can step into real environments together and compare how systems actually operate.

status.inSession: The current cohort is now underway
status.completed: The latest published cohort has concluded

facts.modelValue: Classroom instruction, domestic study visits, and {{speakers}}+ speakers

judgment.title: It compresses strategic judgment.
judgment.description: The value is not just exposure to smart-city terminology, but a clearer sense of what to prioritize, what to question, and what a serious city program should look like.

implementation.title: It is grounded in delivery, not slogans.
implementation.description: The official structure combines expert teaching with institutional references and study visits, which keeps the conversation close to operating reality.

scope.title: It gives leaders a coherent lens across domains.
scope.description: Environment, mobility, energy, governance, people, economy, and livability are treated as part of one city system rather than isolated themes.

investment.title: It signals a meaningful commitment.
investment.description: The time and fee structure place the program closer to an executive commitment than a casual seminar, which changes the level of discussion inside the cohort.

hero.waitlistSuccess: Thank you! We'll notify you when SCL #7 opens.

about.title: A compact course for leaders who need implementation clarity
about.description: The official depa outline positions SCL as a fast executive program covering smart-city project design, digital adoption across public and private contexts, and the practical coordination required to move city initiatives forward.

strategy.title: Sharper strategic framing
strategy.description: A structured view of what makes a smart-city initiative credible, livable, and aligned with city priorities.

delivery.title: Practical delivery references
delivery.description: Examples of how digital systems, operations, and civic infrastructure are applied in real organizations.

network.title: A stronger leadership network
network.description: A cohort environment that connects government, academia, utilities, and private-sector operators.

about.learningSitesTitle: Learning references named in the official article
about.learningSitesDescription: The published SCL #6 announcement highlights site-based learning and organizational references including public infrastructure, digital operations, creative economy institutions, and immersive technology labs.
about.imageCaption: The program is strongest when it reads as a working conversation among leaders, not as a promotional event.

learningSites.site1Desc: National power utility training facility — a live demonstration site for smart grid management, renewable integration, and industrial operations.
learningSites.site2Desc: Provincial waterworks command center with real-time water quality monitoring, predictive maintenance, and citizen service dashboards.
learningSites.site3Desc: Commercial-grade data center supporting cloud services, digital government platforms, and enterprise connectivity.
learningSites.site4Desc: Creative economy campus anchoring Bangkok's design district — where policy, cultural entrepreneurship, and digital tools intersect.
learningSites.site5Desc: University-level immersive technology lab with VR/AR simulation, digital twin prototypes, and applied smart-systems research.

pillarStats.environment: Active in 32 of 37 smart city zones
pillarStats.economy: THB 11.9B+ invested through smart city programs
pillarStats.energy: EGAT + SWOC among SCL study-visit partners

journey.title: Leading the
journey.subtitle: SCL #6 is currently underway. Register your interest to be notified when applications open for SCL #7.

classroom.title: Focused classroom sessions
classroom.description: Core instruction is delivered in a compact format for leaders who need signal, not academic sprawl.

field.title: Domestic study visits
field.description: Participants examine how digital systems and operating models are applied in real Thai organizations.

network.title: Cross-sector cohort exchange
network.description: The cohort mixes public and private participants, which turns the program into a stronger coordination space.

schedule.title: Current published logistics
schedule.description: These details follow depa's official SCL #6 article as verified on April 4, 2026.
schedule.venueValue: Digital Economy Promotion Agency (Building A), 234/431 Soi Ladprao 10, Chatuchak, Bangkok 10900, plus domestic study visits

process.title: Application path
process.description: The official article states that applicants should review the course details, download the form from depa, and submit their materials by email.

review.title: Review the official depa announcement
review.description: Use the source page to confirm the scope, schedule, and whether the program fits your role.

submit.title: Download and email the application
submit.description: The article directs applicants to download the form and submit it through the listed email channels.

attend.title: Join the seven-day cohort
attend.description: Accepted participants move into the short-format executive program and domestic study visits.

fieldwork.title: The real differentiator is the access that keeps opening after the cohort
fieldwork.description: SCL is strongest when the cohort can reassemble around live systems. The network periodically returns to operating environments, technical briefings, and site study sessions so members continue to read smart-city work through implementation, not slogans.

cadence.title: Periodic reconvenings
cadence.description: The network can regroup a few times each year for targeted visits, technical walkthroughs, and focused updates.

access.title: High-value sites
access.description: The draw is access to meaningful places such as EEC environments, industrial solution factories, labs, and operating centers.

context.title: Interpretation with peers
context.description: These visits matter because participants read them with other leaders who understand regulation, procurement, infrastructure, and execution.

journey.sourceNote: Application timing, fee, venue, and schedule are based on depa's official SCL #6 article.
journey.waitlistSuccess: Thank you! We'll notify you when SCL #7 opens.

alumni.title: Executive Alumni
alumni.subtitle: Search across {{count}} leaders from {{cohorts}} official SCL cohorts, with resilient matching for Thai and English names pulled from depa announcement lists.

network.title: The alumni value is not just the directory. It is the continuing access.
network.description: Beyond the course itself, the SCL network periodically reconvenes around study visits and closed-door learning sessions. Members are occasionally brought into smart-city field visits in Bangkok and elsewhere, including high-value sites such as the Eastern Economic Corridor, solution factories, control rooms, and applied technology labs.
network.caption: SCL alumni activity is designed to keep leaders close to real operating environments, not only classroom ideas.

visits.title: Recurring study visits
visits.description: A few times each year, the network can reopen around scientific and technical site visits that extend the learning beyond the original cohort.

access.title: Access to meaningful sites
access.description: The draw is the quality of access: not tourist stops, but factories, labs, city systems, and development zones where real smart-city solutions are being built.

peers.title: A stronger SCL family
peers.description: These trips work because the alumni base already includes public agencies, utilities, universities, and private operators who can interpret what they are seeing together.

alumni.mapCaption: See where our alumni are from — and where we still need to reach.
alumni.updateIntro: If your position or location has changed, please let us know.
alumni.updateBtn: Position or location changed? Click here
alumni.insightsEditorialLead: Two cohorts a year. About eighty leaders per cohort. Five years in — and the network now reaches across most of the country.
alumni.thaiNamesNote: All names in this directory are in Thai only, per the official depa announcement for each cohort.

gallery.subtitle: A visual archive from workshops, site visits, and cohort sessions collected in the SCL media set.

index.title: A data tool for measuring what's actually smart
index.description: An independent scoring framework ranking 37+ Thai smart cities across 7 livability pillars — with interactive exploration tools open to the public.

rankings.title: 37 cities scored and ranked
rankings.description: Composite scores across 7 livability pillars with alpha, beta, and gamma tier classification

allocator.title: Drag to re-rank in real time
allocator.description: Adjust pillar weights on a radar chart and watch cities re-sort instantly

map.title: See where smart cities actually are
map.description: Regional clusters with stacked pillar breakdowns and multiple layer views

reality.title: Official status vs. actual scores
reality.description: Government certification compared against evidence-based outcome ratings

testimonials.title: What alumni say
testimonials.desc: Leaders from across Thailand share their SCL experience

jumpot.name: Jumpot Wannachatrasiri
jumpot.role: Deputy Permanent Secretary, Ministry of Interior
jumpot.quote: An excellent and highly engaging program that expands your perspective with modern policy tools you can apply to develop cities that truly serve their citizens.

chatchawal.name: Chatchawal Benchasiriwong
chatchawal.quote: depa's SCL program systematically builds understanding of Smart City concepts. You can apply the technology to drive urban development effectively — ideal for local leaders who want sustainable, livable cities.

samrit.name: Samrit Liangprasit
samrit.quote: SCL#5 is a quality program offering knowledge from distinguished experts across disciplines, combined with real site visits. The workshops can be directly applied to promote Smart City development.

trisupong.name: Pol.Sub.Lt.Dr. Trisupong Thoopkrachang
trisupong.quote: SCL comprehensively fills your knowledge on smart-city development. It doesn't just build city leaders — it builds livable cities.

wipawan.name: Dr. Wipawan Worawanpricha
wipawan.role: Secretary to the President, Nonthaburi PAO
wipawan.quote: SCL made me understand that Smart City success goes beyond technology. What matters most is the people — if they don't use it, no technology is useful.

wisal.name: Wisal Jirapappan
wisal.quote: SCL#2 enabled us to continuously develop Yala Municipality with Smart City systems, gaining national recognition among local government organizations.

ketsirin.name: Ketsirin Paengsen
ketsirin.role: Head, Mae Moh Livable City Project, EGAT
ketsirin.quote: Developed smart-city leadership with a sustainable vision — from clear long-term planning centered on citizens, to data-driven execution and cross-sector collaboration.

history.title: From Batch #1 to Global Impact
history.2020_desc: Launched with a vision to bridge the gap between technology and urban governance. 40 pioneers joined the first cohort.
history.2022_desc: SCL became a national benchmark, attracting governors and CEOs from across 30+ provinces.
history.2022_desc2: Introduced the Smart City Thailand Index integration, making the program data-driven.
history.2024_desc: Overhauled the curriculum to focus on AI governance and sustainable urban tech.
history.2026_desc: Our largest cohort ever, with 120+ participants representing 50+ provinces.

cta.title: Ready to lead the future of Smart Cities?
cta.desc: SCL #7 applications will open soon. Join the priority waitlist to receive the official brochure and application notification.

loading.alumniRefresh: If this takes too long, please try refreshing the page.

curriculumPlaceholder.title: World-Class Curriculum
curriculumPlaceholder.desc: Our program is designed to provide actionable insights into smart city development, digital economy, and leadership in the age of AI.
curriculumPlaceholder.certificationDesc: Participants who complete all modules and the capstone project receive an official SCL Certification from depa Thailand.

curriculum.title: Six modules built around how smart cities actually get delivered
curriculum.subtitle: Seven weeks. Six modules. Built around the seven smart-city pillars depa works against — and grounded in study visits, not slogans.

1.title: Digital Transformation & Smart City Vision
1.desc: The global landscape of smart-city work, the strategic role of digital infrastructure, and where Thailand sits inside that picture.

2.title: Data-Driven Governance
2.desc: Using analytics and AI to improve urban services, traffic management, and resource allocation — and what to demand from a vendor pitch.

3.title: Leadership & Stakeholder Management
3.desc: Executive skills for leading cross-functional teams, working public-private boundaries, and aligning incentives across agencies.

4.title: Innovation & Smart Economy
4.desc: New business models and economic opportunities in the digital era, with case work from Thai and regional cities.

5.title: Emerging Technologies (AI, IoT, 5G)
5.desc: A working understanding of the technologies shaping next-generation cities and the procurement and integration patterns that matter.

6.title: Capstone: City Transformation Plan
6.desc: A real transformation plan for your city or organization, developed with faculty mentorship and presented at the closing session.

faculty.title: Faculty & Speakers
faculty.subtitle: Industry leaders, mayors, and smart-city pioneers who teach inside the program — not as keynotes, but as working participants in each cohort.
faculty.photoCaption: A rotating faculty of policymakers, mayors, and practitioners teaches each cohort in person.
faculty.partialList: This is a partial list. SCL draws from a rotating faculty of 30+ experts, guest speakers, and city practitioners from global technology firms and smart-city success stories.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything worth knowing about SCL — the program, the application, the people, the outcomes.
faq.contactDesc: We're here to help you lead your city's digital transformation.

methodology.title: Six frameworks. One coherent practice.
methodology.subtitle: SCL does not follow a single academic tradition. It draws from six established frameworks and threads them into a programme built for leaders who need to act, not merely analyse.
methodology.whyDesc: Most smart city failures are not technological. They are methodological — the wrong question was asked first, or the wrong stakeholders were in the room. Framework literacy gives leaders a structured way to diagnose which failure mode they face and how to move out of it.

bmc.name: Business Model Canvas (adapted for cities)
bmc.source: Manetti et al., Energies Vol. 12 No. 24, MDPI (2019)
bmc.insight: Most smart city pilots never scale because they have no viable value-capture mechanism. The Business Model Canvas — when adapted for public-sector contexts — replaces revenue streams with public value flows and treats the resident, not the investor, as the primary stakeholder.
bmc.inSCL: Participants build a city-specific BMC early in the programme to stress-test whether their project has a sustainable logic before any procurement begins.

designThinking.name: Design Thinking
designThinking.source: UNDP Asia-Pacific — Three Design Thinking Lessons for Building Smart Cities
designThinking.insight: Start with the citizen's pain, not the vendor's technology. Prototype at district scale first. Co-create with residents as designers, not beneficiaries. UNDP's approach is a direct counter to the top-down vendor-driven smart city model.
designThinking.inSCL: The empathy-mapping session is a signature SCL moment. Leaders who make city decisions often have not spoken directly with the people those decisions affect.

primer.name: depa Smart City Primer
primer.source: Digital Economy Promotion Agency (depa) — Smart City Primer (official publication)
primer.insight: Thailand's national framework defines seven smart city dimensions that every city seeking depa certification must align to. This is the operating manual for Thai smart city development — not an aspirational document, but a compliance framework with institutional weight.
primer.inSCL: Since depa organises the programme, knowledge of the Primer is a threshold requirement, not optional background reading.

uddc.name: Urban Design Method (UddC)
uddc.source: Urban Design and Development Center, Chulalongkorn University
uddc.insight: Physical space design is not a sidebar to digital infrastructure — it is the infrastructure. Walkability, public realm quality, and genuine stakeholder engagement determine whether smart interventions produce liveable cities or just expensive systems that residents avoid.
uddc.inSCL: Faculty member Dr. Niramon Serisakul (Director, UddC) teaches directly inside the cohort. Her diagnostic: what does the city feel like to walk through?

asean.name: ASEAN Smart Cities Framework
asean.source: ASEAN Smart Cities Network — Smart City Toolkit
asean.insight: Three pillars — Liveable, Competitive, Sustainable. City Action Plans are voluntary and peer-driven, not mandated from a centre. The ASEAN toolkit is organised by urban challenge rather than by product, making it interoperable across governance systems as different as Singapore and Cambodia.
asean.inSCL: Thailand operates within a regional smart city ecosystem. Leaders who understand ASEAN-level frameworks can access regional funding, case studies, and peer networks that nationally-framed programmes cannot reach.

agile.name: Agile Project Management
agile.source: Association for Project Management (APM) — Body of Knowledge
agile.insight: Smart city projects managed as waterfall programmes almost always fail: requirements shift, technology evolves, and political timelines compress. Agile keeps scope flexible, delivers working iterations at every sprint, and maintains genuine stakeholder involvement throughout rather than at approval gates only.
agile.quote: "Agile project management focuses on delivering the maximum value against business priorities in the time and budget allowed." — APM
agile.inSCL: The Capstone project is designed as an Agile sprint. Participants leave with a working deliverable, not a five-year plan.

methodology.weavingDesc: The six frameworks are not taught as separate modules. They follow a deliberate sequence: the BMC establishes the business logic first. Design Thinking generates the citizen diagnosis. The depa Primer translates both into the Thai national context. The UddC method grounds the work in physical reality. The ASEAN framework opens the regional aperture. Agile PM then governs how the Capstone is actually delivered. Each framework addresses a failure mode the others cannot see alone — and together they form a diagnostic toolkit that survives contact with real cities.
methodology.toolsSubtitle: These four instruments were built directly from SCL's frameworks. They are not illustrative examples — they are applied products that city leaders can use today.

sciti.name: Smart City Thailand Index
sciti.desc: A composite scoring system measuring 37+ Thai cities across 7 livability pillars — environment, economy, mobility, energy, governance, people, and living. Gives city leaders a data reference for where their city actually stands, not where it claims to stand.

slic.name: SLIC — Strategic Livability Index for Cities
slic.desc: 163 cities ranked globally using an Adaptive Multi-Pillar Index across five dimensions. Used to position Thai cities against international peers and identify which performance gaps are structural rather than resource-dependent.

ascn.name: ASEAN Smart Cities Network Portal
ascn.desc: A portal mapping Thailand's position in the 26-city ASEAN Smart Cities Network — showing regional peer relationships, shared action plan themes, and bilateral cooperation opportunities across Southeast Asia.

solomon.name: Solomon Islands — Pacific Urban Advisory
solomon.desc: A UN DESA-commissioned engagement applying SCL methodology to a Pacific small-island developing state — demonstrating that the framework transfers well beyond Southeast Asia's urban core.

methodology.aseanSecondSource: ASEAN — Advancing Smart Cities Across ASEAN and Beyond
